english do traditional dances on 1st may.translation: Os ingleses fazem danças tradicionais no dia 1º de maio para dar as boas-vindas ao verão.

definitions.definition: Spinning or rotary motion given to a ball around the vertical axis, as in billiards or bowling.
definitions.example: You can't hit it directly, but maybe if you give it some english.

definitions.definition: An unusual or unexpected interpretation of a text or idea, a spin, a nuance.
A solar year, the time it takes the Earth to complete one revolution of the Sun (between 365.24 and 365.26 days depending on the point of reference).
definitions.example: we moved to this town a year ago;  I quit smoking exactly one year ago

definitions.definition: (by extension) The time it takes for any astronomical object (such as a planet, dwarf planet, small Solar System body, or comet) in direct orbit around a star (such as the Sun) to make one revolution around the star.
definitions.example: Mars goes around the sun once in a Martian year, or 1.88 Earth years.

definitions.definition: A period between set dates that mark a year, from January 1 to December 31 by the Gregorian calendar, from Tishiri 1 to Elul 29 by the Jewish calendar, and from Muharram 1 to Dhu al-Hijjah 29 or 30 by the Islamic calendar.
definitions.example: A normal year has 365 full days, but there are 366 days in a leap year.

definitions.definition: A scheduled part of a calendar year spent in a specific activity.
definitions.example: During this school year I have to get up at 6:30 to catch the bus.

definitions.definition: A Julian year, exactly 365.25 days, represented by "a".
A level or grade in school or college.
definitions.example: Every second-year student must select an area of specialization.

definitions.definition: The proportion of a creature's lifespan equivalent to one year of an average human lifespan (see also dog year).
definitions.example: Geneticists have created baker's yeast that can live to 800 in yeast years.

definitions.definition: A sequence of rhythmic steps or movements usually performed to music, for pleasure or as a form of social interaction.
A social gathering where dancing is the main activity.
A normally horizontal stripe called a fess that has been modified to zig-zag across the center of a coat of arms from dexter to sinister.
A genre of modern music characterised by sampled beats, repetitive rhythms and few lyrics.
The art, profession, and study of dancing.
A piece of music with a particular dance rhythm.
A battle of wits, especially one commonly fought between two rivals.
definitions.example: So how much longer are we gonna do this dance?

definitions.definition: To move with rhythmic steps or movements, especially in time to music.
definitions.example: I danced with her all night long.

definitions.definition: To leap or move lightly and rapidly.
definitions.example: His eyes danced with pleasure as he spoke.   She accused her political opponent of dancing around the issue instead of confronting it.

definitions.definition: To perform the steps to.
definitions.example: Have you ever danced the tango?

definitions.definition: To cause to dance, or move nimbly or merrily about.
To make love or have sex.
definitions.example: You make me feel like dancing.

definitions.definition: One of four seasons, traditionally the second, marked by the longest and typically hottest days of the year due to the inclination of the Earth and thermal lag. Typically regarded as being from June 21 to September 22 or 23 in parts of the USA, the months of June, July and August in the United Kingdom and the months of December, January and February in the Southern Hemisphere.
definitions.example: the heat of summer

definitions.definition: Year; used to give the age of a person, usually a young one.
definitions.example: He was barely eighteen summers old.

definitions.definition: Someone with light, pinkish skin that has a blue undertone, light hair and eyes, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
To spend the summer, as in a particular place on holiday.
definitions.example: We like to summer in the Mediterranean.

definitions.definition: (auxiliary) A syntactic marker.
To perform; to execute.
definitions.example: All you ever do is surf the Internet. What will you do this afternoon?

definitions.definition: To cause, make (someone) (do something).
To suffice.
definitions.example: it’s not the best broom, but it will have to do;  this will do me, thanks.

definitions.definition: To be reasonable or acceptable.
definitions.example: It simply will not do to have dozens of children running around such a quiet event.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To have (as an effect).
definitions.example: The fresh air did him some good.

definitions.definition: To fare, perform (well or poorly).
definitions.example: Our relationship isn't doing very well;  how do you do?

definitions.definition: (chiefly in questions) To have as one's job.
definitions.example: What does Bob do? — He's a plumber.

definitions.definition: To perform the tasks or actions associated with (something).
definitions.example: "Don't forget to do your report" means something quite different depending on whether you're a student or a programmer.

definitions.definition: To cook.
definitions.example: I'll just do some eggs.

definitions.definition: To travel in, to tour, to make a circuit of.
definitions.example: Let’s do New York also.

definitions.definition: To treat in a certain way.
To work for or on, by way of caring for, looking after, preparing, cleaning, keeping in order, etc.
To act or behave in a certain manner; to conduct oneself.
To spend (time) in jail. (See also do time)
definitions.example: I did five years for armed robbery.

definitions.definition: To impersonate or depict.
definitions.example: They really laughed when he did Clinton, with a perfect accent and a leer.

definitions.definition: (with 'a' and the name of a person, place, event, etc.) To copy or emulate the actions or behaviour that is associated with the person or thing mentioned.
definitions.example: He did a Henry VIII and got married six times.

definitions.definition: To kill.
To deal with for good and all; to finish up; to undo; to ruin; to do for.
To punish for a misdemeanor.
definitions.example: He got done for speeding.

definitions.definition: To have sex with. (See also do it)
To cheat or swindle.
definitions.example: That guy just did me out of two hundred bucks!

definitions.definition: To convert into a certain form; especially, to translate.
definitions.example: the novel has just been done into English;  I'm going to do this play into a movie

definitions.definition: To finish.
To work as a domestic servant (with for).
(auxiliary) Used to form the present progressive of verbs.
To cash or to advance money for, as a bill or note.
(ditransitive) To make or provide.
definitions.example: Could you do me a burger with mayonnaise instead of ketchup?

definitions.definition: To injure (one's own body part).
To take drugs.
definitions.example: I do cocaine.

definitions.definition: (in the form be doing [somewhere]) To exist with a purpose or for a reason.
definitions.example: What's that car doing in our swimming pool?

definitions.definition: (of food) Ready, fully cooked.
definitions.example: As soon as the potatoes are done we can sit down and eat.

definitions.definition: Having completed or finished an activity.
definitions.example: He pushed his empty plate away, sighed and pronounced "I am done."

definitions.definition: Being exhausted or fully spent.
definitions.example: When the water is done we will only be able to go on for a few days.

definitions.definition: Without hope or prospect of completion or success.
definitions.example: He is done, after three falls there is no chance he will be able to finish.

definitions.definition: Fashionable, socially acceptable, tasteful.
definitions.example: I can't believe he just walked up and spoke to her like that, those kind of things just aren't done!

definitions.definition: The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
The external part of the organ of hearing, the auricle.
A police informant.
The sense of hearing; the perception of sounds; skill or good taste in listening to music.
definitions.example: a good ear for music

definitions.definition: The privilege of being kindly heard; favour; attention.
That which resembles in shape or position the ear of an animal; a prominence or projection on an object, usually for support or attachment; a lug; a handle.
definitions.example: the ears of a tub, skillet, or dish;   The ears of a boat are outside kneepieces near the bow.

definitions.definition: An acroterium.
A crossette.
To take in with the ears; to hear.
To hold by the ears.
To be strong; to have power (over).
(auxiliary) To be able; can.
To be able to go.
(modal auxiliary verb, defective) To have permission to, be allowed. Used in granting permission and in questions to make polite requests.
definitions.example: you may smoke outside;  may I sit there?

definitions.definition: (modal auxiliary verb, defective) Expressing a present possibility; possibly.
definitions.example: he may be lying;  Schrödinger's cat may or may not be in the box

definitions.definition: (subjunctive present, defective) Expressing a wish (with present subjunctive effect).
definitions.example: may you win;  may the weather be sunny

definitions.definition: Used in modesty, courtesy, or concession, or to soften a question or remark.

to be strong; to have power (over).\n(au.translation:
Ser forte; ter poder (sobre).
(auxiliar) Para poder; can.

o be strong; to have power (over).\n(aux.translation:
ser forte; ter poder (sobre).
(auxiliar) Para poder; ca

definitions.definition: Not different or other; not another or others; not different as regards self; selfsame; identical.
definitions.example: Are you the same person who phoned me yesterday?

definitions.definition: Lacking variety from; indistinguishable.
definitions.example: It took all night to find our hotel room, as we forgot our room number and each door looked the same.

definitions.definition: Similar, alike.
definitions.example: You have the same hair I do!

definitions.definition: Used to express the unity of an object or person which has various different descriptions or qualities.
definitions.example: Round here it can be cloudy and sunny even in the same day.

definitions.definition: A reply of confirmation of identity.
The identical thing, ditto.
definitions.example: It's the same everywhere.

definitions.definition: Something similar, something of the identical type.
definitions.example: She's having apple pie? I'll have the same.   You two are just the same.

definitions.definition: It or them, without a connotation of similarity.
definitions.example: Light valve suspensions and films containing UV absorbers and light valves containing the same (US Patent 5,467,217)

definitions.definition: (Indian English, common) It or them, as above, meaning the last object mentioned, mainly as complement: on the same, for the same.
definitions.example: My picture/photography blog...kindly give me your reviews on the same.

definitions.definition: Indicates the speaker's strong approval or agreement with the previous material.
The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
definitions.example: She stood with her right arm extended and her palm forward to indicate “Stop!”

definitions.definition: The extended portion of the upper limb, from the shoulder to the elbow.
definitions.example: The arm and forearm are parts of the upper limb in the human body.

definitions.definition: A limb, or locomotive or prehensile organ, of an invertebrate animal.
definitions.example: the arms of an octopus

definitions.definition: A long, narrow, more or less rigid part of an object extending from the main part or centre of the object, such as the arm of an armchair, a crane, a pair of spectacles or a pair of compasses.
definitions.example: The robot arm reached out and placed the part on the assembly line.

definitions.definition: A bay or inlet off a main body of water.
definitions.example: Shelburne Bay is an arm of Lake Champlain.

definitions.definition: A branch of an organization.
definitions.example: the cavalry arm of the military service

definitions.definition: Power; might; strength; support.
definitions.example: the arm of the law

definitions.definition: A pitcher
definitions.example: The team needs to sign another arm in the offseason.

definitions.definition: One of the two parts of a chromosome.
A group of patients in a medical trial.
To take by the arm; to take up in one's arms.
To clean (a surface) by means of a stroking motion of a broom or brush.
definitions.example: to sweep a floor, the street, or a chimney

definitions.definition: To move through a (horizontal) arc or similar long stroke.
definitions.example: The offended countess swept out of the ballroom.

definitions.definition: To search (a place) methodically.
To travel quickly.
To play a sweep shot.
To brush the ice in front of a moving stone, causing it to travel farther and to curl less.
To move something in a long sweeping motion, as a broom.
To win (a series) without drawing or losing any of the games in that series.
To defeat (a team) in a series without drawing or losing any of the games in that series.
To remove something abruptly and thoroughly.
definitions.example: She swept the peelings off the table onto the floor.

definitions.definition: To brush against or over; to rub lightly along.
definitions.example: Their long descending train, / With rubies edg'd and sapphires, swept the plain.

definitions.definition: To carry with a long, swinging, or dragging motion; hence, to carry in a stately or proud fashion.
To strike with a long stroke.
To row with one oar to either the port or starboard side.
To draw or drag something over.
definitions.example: to sweep the bottom of a river with a net

definitions.definition: To pass over, or traverse, with the eye or with an instrument of observation.
definitions.example: to sweep the heavens with a telescope

definitions.definition: (including) to vacuum a carpet or rug

this is my last slice of cake, i promise.translation: Esta é a minha última fatia de bolo, eu prometo!

definitions.definition: Something being indicated that is here; one of these.
Referring to the current context in a programming environment in C-like languages.
To the degree or extent indicated.
definitions.example: Do we need this many recommendations?

definitions.definition: The thing, item, etc. being indicated.
definitions.example: This isn't the item that I ordered.

definitions.definition: Indicates the speaker's strong approval or agreement with the previous material.
An oath or affirmation; a vow
definitions.example: if I make a promise, I always stick to it;  he broke his promise

definitions.definition: A transaction between two persons whereby the first person undertakes in the future to render some service or gift to the second person or devotes something valuable now and here to his use
Reason to expect improvement or success; potential
definitions.example: She shows great promise as an actress.

definitions.definition: A placeholder object representing the eventual result of an asynchronous operation
Bestowal or fulfillment of what is promised
To commit to (some action or outcome), or to assure (a person) of such commitment; to make an oath or vow.
definitions.example: He promised to never return to this town again.

definitions.definition: To give grounds for expectation, especially of something good.
definitions.example: The clouds promise rain.

definitions.definition: Final, ultimate, coming after all others of its kind.
definitions.example: “Eyes Wide Shut” was the last film to be directed by Stanley Kubrick.

definitions.definition: Most recent, latest, last so far.
definitions.example: I have received your note dated the 17th last, and am responding to say that [...]

definitions.definition: Farthest of all from a given quality, character, or condition; most unlikely, or least preferable.
definitions.example: He is the last person to be accused of theft.

definitions.definition: Being the only one remaining of its class.
definitions.example: Japan is the last empire.

definitions.definition: Supreme; highest in degree; utmost.
Lowest in rank or degree.
definitions.example: the last prize

definitions.definition: Most recently.
definitions.example: When we last met, he was based in Toronto.

definitions.definition: (sequence) after everything else; finally
definitions.example: I'll go last.

definitions.definition: To such an extent or degree; to the same extent or degree.
definitions.example: It's not as well made, but it's twice as expensive.

definitions.definition: Considered to be, in relation to something else; in the relation (specified).
definitions.example: 1865, The Act of Suicide as Distinct from the Crime of Self-Murder: A Sermon

definitions.definition: For example; for instance. (Compare such as.)
Introducing a basis of comparison, with an object in the objective case.
definitions.example: They're big as houses.

definitions.definition: In the role of.
definitions.example: He was never seen as the boss, but rather as a friend.

definitions.definition: In the (same) way or manner that; to the (same) degree that.
definitions.example: As you wish, my lord!

definitions.definition: At the time that; during the time when:
Being that, considering that, because, since.
definitions.example: As it’s too late, I quit.

definitions.definition: Introducing a comparison with a hypothetical state (+ subjunctive, or with the verb elided): as though, as if.
Functioning as a relative conjunction, and sometimes like a relative pronoun: that, which, who. (See usage notes.)
definitions.example: He had the same problem as she did getting the lock open.

definitions.definition: (possibly obsolete) Than.

his is my last slice of cake, i prom.translation: esta é a minha última fatia de bolo, eu prometo

this is my last slice of cake.translation: Esta é a minha última fatia de bolo

definitions.definition: A rich, sweet dessert food, typically made of flour, sugar and eggs and baked in an oven, and often covered in icing.
A small mass of baked dough, especially a thin loaf from unleavened dough.
definitions.example: a johnnycake

definitions.definition: A thin wafer-shaped mass of fried batter; a griddlecake or pancake.
definitions.example: buckwheat cakes

definitions.definition: A block of any of various dense materials.
definitions.example: a cake of sand

definitions.definition: A trivially easy task or responsibility; from a piece of cake.
Money.
Used to describe the doctrine of having one's cake and eating it too.
A buttock, especially one that is exceptionally plump.
definitions.example: Mmm, I'd like to cut me some of that cake!

definitions.definition: Coat (something) with a crust of solid material.
definitions.example: His shoes are caked with mud.

definitions.definition: To form into a cake, or mass.
That which is thin and broad.
A thin, broad piece cut off.
definitions.example: Jim was munching on a slice of toast.

definitions.definition: An amount of anything.
A piece of pizza.
A snack consisting of pastry with savoury filling.
definitions.example: I bought a ham and cheese slice at the service station.

definitions.definition: A broad, thin piece of plaster.
A knife with a thin, broad blade for taking up or serving fish; also, a spatula for spreading anything, as paint or ink.
A salver, platter, or tray.

A plate of iron with a handle, forming a kind of chisel, or a spadelike implement, variously proportioned, and used for various purposes, as for stripping the planking from a vessel's side, for cutting blubber from a whale, or for stirring a fire of coals; a slice bar; a peel; a fire shovel.
One of the wedges by which the cradle and the ship are lifted clear of the building blocks to prepare for launching.
A removable sliding bottom to a galley.
A shot that (for the right-handed player) curves unintentionally to the right. See fade, hook, draw
Any of a class of heavy cakes or desserts made in a tray and cut out into squarish slices.
A section of image taken of an internal organ using MRI (magnetic resonance imaging), CT (computed tomography), or various forms of x-ray.
A hawk's or falcon's dropping which squirts at an angle other than vertical. (See mute.)
A contiguous portion of an array.
To cut into slices.
definitions.example: Slice the cheese thinly.

definitions.definition: To cut with an edge utilizing a drawing motion.
definitions.example: The knife left sliced his arm.

definitions.definition: To clear (e.g. a fire, or the grate bars of a furnace) by means of a slice bar.
To hit the shuttlecock with the racket at an angle, causing it to move sideways and downwards.
To hit a shot that slices (travels from left to right for a right-handed player).
To angle the blade so that it goes too deeply into the water when starting to take a stroke.
To kick the ball so that it goes in an unintended direction, at too great an angle or too high.
To hit the ball with a stroke that causes a spin, resulting in the ball swerving or staying low after a bounce.
Having the properties of a slice knot.
Used to express surprise, shock or amazement.
definitions.example: My, what big teeth you have!
